events.title: HYROX Sydney
events.description: The World Series of Fitness Racing returns to Sydney. 8 workout stations, 8 x 1km runs. Individual and doubles divisions available.
events.fullDescription: HYROX Sydney is the pinnacle of fitness racing in Australia. Competitors complete 8 x 1km runs each separated by a functional workout station — SkiErg, Sled Push, Sled Pull, Burpee Broad Jumps, Rowing, Farmers Carry, Sandbag Lunges, and Wall Balls. Whether you're racing solo, in doubles, or as a relay team, this is the event that defines your fitness.
events.workoutDescription: 8 functional workout stations each preceded by a 1km run: SkiErg (1000m), Sled Push (50m), Sled Pull (50m), Burpee Broad Jumps (80m), Rowing (1000m), Farmers Carry (200m), Sandbag Lunges (100m), Wall Balls (100 reps). Total: 8km running + stations.
events.transferPolicy: Category transfers permitted up to 14 days before race day. A $15 admin fee applies.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds issued. Deferrals to the following year's event available up to 30 days before race day for a $25 fee.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, HYROX timing chip, official race bib, post-race recovery bag, and access to the expo floor.
events.optionalExtras: Official HYROX race kit ($65), professional race photos ($45), merchandise bundle ($89).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 10+ receive 10% off. Corporate team packages available — contact events@hyrox.com.au.
events.charityComponent: HYROX Sydney proudly supports the Victor Chang Cardiac Research Institute. Participants can opt in to raise funds via their personal fundraising page.
events.prizeStructure: Cash and trophies awarded to top 3 overall in each division. Age group winners receive HYROX trophies and prize vouchers.
events.ceremonyLocation: Main Stage, Sydney Olympic Park — on-site at the event
events.specialAwards: Segment KOM/QOM, Volunteer of the Year, Community Spirit Award, Strava Challenge Winner
events.expoDetails: Free entry for all spectators and non-participants. Features brand activations, product demos, nutrition stands, and a kids fitness zone.
events.bibCollectionInfo: 11 Jul 2026 (Day Before) — Sydney Olympic Park Exhibitor Hall, 10:00–18:00. Race day pick-up available from 05:30 at the registration tent.
events.athleteBriefing: Online briefing video available from 5 Jul 2026 at hyrox.com/sydney-briefing. On-site briefing for Relay teams only at 06:30 on race day.
events.accessibilityInfo: Sydney Olympic Park is fully wheelchair accessible. Accessible parking bays available in Car Park 4. Contact us for specific accommodation needs.
events.parkingInfo: Car Park 4 & 6 open from 05:30. $15 flat rate. Train: Olympic Park Station (T7 line) is a 5-minute walk.
events.bagDropInfo: Complimentary bag drop available at the event village. Open from 06:00. No responsibility taken for valuables.
events.additionalNotes: Spectator entry is free. Bring your own water bottle — refill stations are located throughout the course. Dogs permitted in outdoor areas on a lead.

events.title: HYROX Melbourne
events.description: Melbourne's premier fitness racing event. Test your limits across 8 functional workout stations connected by 1km runs.
events.fullDescription: HYROX Melbourne brings the world's leading fitness race to the heart of Victoria. The Melbourne Convention Centre is transformed into an incredible race venue where thousands of athletes push themselves through 8km of running and 8 signature workout stations. All fitness levels welcome — from first-timers to season veterans.
events.workoutDescription: 8 functional workout stations each preceded by a 1km run: SkiErg (1000m), Sled Push (50m), Sled Pull (50m), Burpee Broad Jumps (80m), Rowing (1000m), Farmers Carry (200m), Sandbag Lunges (100m), Wall Balls (100 reps). Total: 8km running + stations.
events.transferPolicy: Category transfers permitted up to 14 days before race day. A $15 admin fee applies.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds issued. Deferrals available up to 30 days before race day for a $25 fee.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, HYROX timing chip, official race bib, post-race recovery bag, and expo access.
events.optionalExtras: Official HYROX race kit ($65), professional race photos ($45), merchandise bundle ($89).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 10+ receive 10% off. Corporate packages available.
events.charityComponent: This year's charity partner is Beyond Blue. Athletes can fundraise through their personal race profile.
events.prizeStructure: Cash prizes and trophies for top 3 in each open division. Age group trophies for all category winners.
events.ceremonyLocation: Main Stage, Melbourne Convention Centre — on-site
events.specialAwards: Fastest Female, Fastest Male, Best Doubles Team, Community Spirit Award
events.expoDetails: Free expo open to all — brand activations, nutrition demos, gear stalls, and athlete meet-and-greets.
events.bibCollectionInfo: 17 Apr 2026 (Day Before) — Convention Centre Foyer, 10:00–18:00. Race day pick-up from 05:30.
events.athleteBriefing: Online briefing available from 11 Apr 2026 at hyrox.com/melbourne-briefing. No mandatory on-site briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Melbourne Convention Centre is fully accessible. Wheelchair-accessible parking available in the Convention Centre car park.
events.parkingInfo: Convention Centre car park open from 05:30. $20 flat rate. Tram: Routes 96 and 109 stop nearby.
events.bagDropInfo: Free bag drop at the event village from 06:00. Secure storage provided.
events.additionalNotes: Spectator entry is free. Café and food stalls on-site from 06:30.

events.title: CrossFit Torian Pro
events.description: Elite CrossFit competition featuring Australia's top athletes. Sanctioned event with Games qualification spots.
events.fullDescription: CrossFit Torian Pro is Australia's premier elite CrossFit competition and an official CrossFit Games qualifier. Watch and compete alongside the nation's fittest athletes across two days of demanding programmed events. Sanctioned by CrossFit HQ with direct qualification spots to the CrossFit Games on the line.
events.streetAddress: Merivale St & Glenelg St, South Brisbane
events.workoutDescription: Workouts are programmed and revealed at the event. Expect a mix of barbell cycling, gymnastics, monostructural conditioning, and odd-object movements across 4–5 competition events over the weekend.
events.transferPolicy: No transfers between divisions. Name transfers permitted up to 21 days before the event for a $20 fee.
events.refundPolicy: 50% refund available up to 60 days before the event. No refunds within 60 days.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Competition entry, event t-shirt, athlete bag, timing and judging for all workouts, post-event athlete dinner (Friday night).
events.optionalExtras: Spectator passes ($35/day), VIP hospitality ($120/day), video highlight package ($75).
events.groupDiscount: Affiliate gym discount: 5% off for affiliates entering 3 or more athletes. Contact admin@torianpro.com.
events.prizeStructure: Prize money awarded to top 5 in the Individual Elite division. All other divisions receive trophies and podium recognition.
events.specialAwards: Spirit of the Sport Award, Best Affiliate Representation, Crowd Favourite
events.expoDetails: Full vendor floor open both days. CrossFit-affiliated brands, supplement companies, apparel, and equipment demos. Free entry for spectators.
events.bibCollectionInfo: Athlete check-in: 20 Mar 2026 (Friday evening) 17:00–20:00 at the Convention Centre main entrance. Mandatory check-in required.
events.athleteBriefing: Mandatory athlete briefing: 20 Mar 2026 at 19:00. Workouts revealed at briefing. Attendance required.
events.accessibilityInfo: Brisbane Convention Centre is fully accessible. Notify the organiser in advance for specific requirements.
events.parkingInfo: Wilson Parking on Glenelg St. $15 early bird rate before 08:30. Public transport: South Brisbane station is a 5-minute walk.
events.bagDropInfo: Athlete bag storage provided in the warm-up area. Accessible during your division's warm-up window only.
events.additionalNotes: All athletes must present valid CrossFit ID at check-in. CrossFit Games qualification rules apply to the Individual Elite division.

events.title: Sydney Marathon
events.description: Australia's largest marathon through the heart of Sydney. Marathon, half marathon, and 10km options available.
events.fullDescription: The Sydney Marathon is one of the world's great city marathons, taking runners on an iconic journey past the Opera House, across the Harbour Bridge, through the CBD and into the park-lined streets of Moore Park. An Abbott World Marathon Major qualifier, this race attracts both elite international runners and thousands of recreational participants.
events.streetAddress: Start: Milsons Point, Finish: Moore Park
events.workoutDescription: Road race. Marathon starts at Milsons Point and crosses the iconic Sydney Harbour Bridge before winding through the CBD and finishing at Moore Park. Half marathon and 10km courses are point-to-point through Sydney's eastern suburbs.
events.transferPolicy: Distance transfers permitted up to 6 weeks before race day for a $20 fee. Name transfers not available.
events.refundPolicy: Deferral to 2027 available for a $30 fee up to 8 weeks before the event. No refunds.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race food and drink, finisher t-shirt (marathon and half marathon only).
events.optionalExtras: Official race photos ($55), gear check bag ($10), training plans from $29.
events.groupDiscount: Corporate and running club entries: groups of 20+ save 15%. Contact corporate@sydneymarathon.com.
events.charityComponent: Run for a cause — charity bib holders receive guaranteed entry. Over 40 official charity partners. Fundraising minimum applies.
events.prizeStructure: Prize money to top 3 male and female marathon finishers. Age group trophies for all distances. Masters categories recognised.
events.ceremonyLocation: Finish Line Stage, Moore Park — on-site
events.specialAwards: Course Record Bonus ($10,000), Masters Champion (M/F), First Australian Finisher
events.expoDetails: Sydney Marathon Expo runs 18–19 Sep at the International Convention Centre, Darling Harbour. Mandatory bib collection. Free entry for all.
events.bibCollectionInfo: Mandatory at the Sydney Marathon Expo: 18 Sep (10:00–19:00) or 19 Sep (09:00–17:00), ICC Darling Harbour. Race day collection not available.
events.athleteBriefing: Full race day information and course maps available at sydneymarathon.com/race-guide. No mandatory on-site briefing.
events.minAge: 18+ for Marathon. 16+ for Half Marathon. 12+ for 10km.
events.accessibilityInfo: Wheelchair division available for the marathon. Handcycle and push-rim categories. Contact accessibility@sydneymarathon.com.
events.parkingInfo: No parking at the start. Train to Milsons Point Station. Finish area: Moore Park — bus services from the CBD throughout the morning.
events.bagDropInfo: Free gear check at Moore Park finish village. Drop off at the start line collection trucks. Retrieved by bib number.
events.additionalNotes: Course is certified by Athletics Australia. Marathon is an Abbott World Marathon Majors qualifier. Personal pacers not permitted.

events.title: Melbourne Marathon Festival
events.description: Run through Melbourne's iconic streets and parks. Full marathon, half marathon, 10km and 5km distances.
events.fullDescription: The Melbourne Marathon Festival is Victoria's largest running event, attracting over 30,000 participants across four distances. The iconic course takes you through Melbourne's famous laneways, past the MCG, through the Botanical Gardens and finishes on the hallowed turf of the MCG — one of the most memorable finishing lines in world running.
events.streetAddress: Start: Shrine of Remembrance, Finish: MCG
events.workoutDescription: Fully certified road race. Marathon starts at the Shrine of Remembrance and winds through Melbourne's inner suburbs, St Kilda, Port Melbourne and Richmond before finishing inside the MCG. The 5km is a flat inner-city loop perfect for first-timers.
events.transferPolicy: Distance upgrades/downgrades available up to 6 weeks before the event for a $15 admin fee.
events.refundPolicy: Event insurance available at registration for $12. Otherwise no refunds. Deferrals to 2027 for $25 up to 30 days out.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race festival access, finisher shirt (marathon only).
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt for all distances ($30), official photos ($49), training programs from $19.
events.groupDiscount: Run clubs of 15+ receive 10% discount. Contact groups@melbournemarathon.com.au.
events.charityComponent: Official charity program with 50+ partner charities. Charity bib holders receive guaranteed entry with a fundraising minimum of $500 (marathon).
events.prizeStructure: Prize money for top 3 male/female marathon finishers. Age group trophies across all distances. Open and masters categories.
events.specialAwards: First Melbourne resident finisher, Oldest Finisher, Best Costume, Running Club of the Year
events.expoDetails: Melbourne Marathon Expo at Melbourne Park, 9–10 Oct. Hundreds of exhibitors, product launches, free seminars and activities. Mandatory bib pick-up.
events.bibCollectionInfo: Melbourne Park, Gate 4: 9 Oct (10:00–19:00), 10 Oct (09:00–17:00). Race day bib collection available for 5km only.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide available at melbournemarathon.com.au/race-guide from 28 Sep 2026. No mandatory briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Wheelchair and handcycle divisions available. Accessible facilities throughout the course. Contact accessibility@melbournemarathon.com.au.
events.parkingInfo: MCG precinct parking available but limited. Recommend public transport — City Loop and Richmond trains servicing all start/finish areas.
events.bagDropInfo: Free gear check at the MCG. Drop-off at the start, collected by bib number at the finish festival.
events.additionalNotes: Pacer groups available for 3:00, 3:30, 4:00, 4:30 and 5:00 marathon targets. Dogs and prams not permitted on the course.

events.title: Down Under CrossFit Championship
events.description: Open CrossFit competition for all skill levels. Scaled, RX, and Masters divisions. Team and individual events.
events.fullDescription: The Down Under CrossFit Championship is one of Queensland's most inclusive and fun CrossFit competitions. Athletes of all levels compete across Scaled, RX, and Masters divisions in a festive atmosphere at CrossFit GC. Whether you're chasing a podium or just want to test yourself in a competition environment for the first time, this event has a division for you.
events.workoutDescription: 3 workouts programmed across the day. Workouts will test a variety of movements including barbell work, gymnastics, and monostructural cardio. Scaled movements provided for all workouts. Full movement standards released 2 weeks prior.
events.transferPolicy: Athlete name transfers permitted up to 2 weeks before the event for a $10 fee. Division changes subject to availability.
events.refundPolicy: Full refund available up to 60 days before the event. 50% refund up to 30 days. No refund within 30 days.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Competition entry, event t-shirt, judge allocation, post-event BBQ and drinks.
events.optionalExtras: Extra spectator tickets ($15 each), event hoodie ($65), photo package ($40).
events.groupDiscount: Affiliate discount: 10% off for groups of 5+ from the same box. Contact gc@downunderchamps.com.
events.charityComponent: Gold coin donation on the day supports the Gold Coast Lifeline. Target: $2,000.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies and sponsor prizes for top 3 in each division. Overall champion receives a trophy and $500 store credit.
events.specialAwards: Crowd Favourite, Best Scaled Athlete, Most Improved
events.bibCollectionInfo: Check-in on race day from 07:00 at the CrossFit GC front desk. Bring photo ID.
events.athleteBriefing: Athlete briefing at 07:45 on race day. Attendance mandatory. Movement standards Q&A included.
events.accessibilityInfo: Ground-level venue. Accessible facilities available. Contact the organiser for specific needs.
events.parkingInfo: Free on-site parking at CrossFit GC. Additional street parking on Kortum Drive.
events.bagDropInfo: Athlete bag area inside the gym. Not supervised — valuables at your own risk.
events.additionalNotes: Spectators welcome — free entry. Food trucks on-site from 08:00. Bring your own water bottle.

events.title: Gold Coast Marathon
events.description: Australia's premier road race along the stunning Gold Coast beaches. Known for fast, flat courses.
events.fullDescription: The Gold Coast Marathon is renowned as Australia's fastest marathon course — an IAAF Gold Label Road Race that consistently produces Australian and Oceanian records. Run along the spectacular Gold Coast Broadwater and Surfers Paradise, taking in ocean views the entire way. The 5.7km family fun run is one of the most popular in the country.
events.workoutDescription: Certified flat road race along the Gold Coast Broadwater and beachfront. Marathon and half marathon are out-and-back courses. 10km and 5.7km are point-to-point. Course PB-friendly.
events.transferPolicy: Distance transfers available up to 8 weeks before the event. $20 admin fee.
events.refundPolicy: Deferrals to 2027 available up to 8 weeks before the event for a $30 fee. No refunds.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race refreshments.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($35), professional photos ($50), commemorative magazine ($15).
events.groupDiscount: Running clubs of 20+ save 10%. Contact groups@goldcoastmarathon.com.au.
events.charityComponent: Official charity partner: Starlight Children's Foundation. Charity bibs available with $750 fundraising minimum (marathon).
events.prizeStructure: Prize money for top 5 male/female marathon finishers. Age group awards across all distances.
events.specialAwards: Course Record Bonus, First Queensland Resident, Age Group Champion
events.expoDetails: Gold Coast Marathon Expo at the Broadwater Parklands event village, 3–4 Jul. Bib collection, merchandise, sponsor activations.
events.bibCollectionInfo: Expo: 3 Jul (10:00–18:00), 4 Jul (08:00–17:00). Race day collection for 5.7km only from 06:00.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide published at goldcoastmarathon.com.au/race-guide. No mandatory on-site briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Wheelchair and handcycle divisions in the marathon. Fully accessible course and facilities.
events.parkingInfo: Event parking at Broadwater Parklands and nearby streets. Arrive before 05:00 for best access. Gold Coast light rail nearby.
events.bagDropInfo: Free gear check at the start/finish area. Identified by bib number.
events.additionalNotes: IAAF Gold Label Road Race. Course records eligible for World Athletics certification. Pacer program for marathon and half marathon.

events.title: Tribal Clash Australia
events.description: Team functional fitness competition on the beach. 6-person teams battle through ocean swims, runs, and workouts.
events.fullDescription: Tribal Clash Australia is the most unique team fitness competition in the country. 6-person co-ed teams take on a full day of unknown challenges on and around Bondi Beach — from ocean swims and beach sprints to log carries, rope pulls, and heavy lifting. No programming preview, no pre-built strategy. Just you and your tribe.
events.workoutDescription: Unknown workouts revealed on race day. Expect: ocean swim relay, beach run, log carry, sandbag medley, rope pull, team lifting events, and a mystery endurance challenge. All team members must complete every event together.
events.transferPolicy: Team member substitutions permitted up to 7 days before the event. No division changes.
events.refundPolicy: 50% refund available up to 60 days before the event. No refunds within 60 days. Team transfer to 2027 available.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Team entry for all 6 members, event t-shirts, post-event BBQ and drinks, team photo, timing.
events.optionalExtras: Team custom singlets ($35 each), additional BBQ tickets for guests ($25), photo package ($120/team).
events.prizeStructure: Trophies and prize packs for top 3 teams in each division. Champions receive Tribal Clash trophies and sponsor prizes.
events.specialAwards: Best Team Name, Best Team Kit, Spirit of the Clash
events.bibCollectionInfo: Team check-in on race day from 06:00 at the Bondi Pavilion. One team captain must check in for all members.
events.athleteBriefing: Team captains briefing at 06:45 — mandatory attendance. Race day schedule and safety briefing covered.
events.accessibilityInfo: Due to the nature of beach events, full accessibility cannot be guaranteed. Contact organiser to discuss options.
events.parkingInfo: Very limited parking at Bondi. Strongly recommend public transport — Bus 333 from the CBD stops directly at the beach.
events.bagDropInfo: Team area allocated on the beach for gear. Not supervised. Keep valuables secured.
events.additionalNotes: All team members must be able to swim 200m in open water. Please indicate any non-swimmers at registration. Ocean conditions may alter the programme.

events.title: City2Surf
events.description: The world's largest fun run. 14km from Sydney CBD to Bondi Beach. A must-do Sydney running event.
events.fullDescription: City2Surf is the world's largest fun run — a Sydney institution since 1971. Over 80,000 participants run, jog, walk, and skip 14km from the CBD through the leafy eastern suburbs and over the famous Heartbreak Hill to finish on the golden sands of Bondi Beach. Whether you're chasing a time or just joining the party, this event is unmissable.
events.streetAddress: Start: Hyde Park North, Sydney CBD
events.workoutDescription: Point-to-point 14km road run from Hyde Park in the CBD to Bondi Beach. Features the notorious Heartbreak Hill at Rose Bay — a 2km climb that separates the field. Predominantly flat otherwise with ocean views on the descent into Bondi.
events.transferPolicy: Name transfers not available. Entry deferrals to 2027 for $15 up to 14 days before the event.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferral available for $15 up to 2 weeks before the event.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Race bib, timing chip, finisher medal, post-race refreshments at Bondi Beach.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($30), official race photos ($45), guaranteed start wave upgrade ($20).
events.groupDiscount: Corporate teams of 10+ — contact corporate@city2surf.com.au for group packages.
events.charityComponent: Australia's biggest fundraising run event. Choose from 200+ charity partners. No fundraising minimum required.
events.prizeStructure: Prize money for top 10 male and female elite finishers. Age group trophies.
events.specialAwards: Best Costume, Fastest Charity Fundraiser, Community Hero Award
events.bibCollectionInfo: Mail delivery available for entries before 20 Jul. Otherwise collect at designated pick-up points in the CBD from 7–8 Aug.
events.athleteBriefing: Full race day guide at city2surf.com.au. No mandatory briefing. Start wave times on your race bib.
events.minAge: 12+ (under 17 must be accompanied by an adult)
events.accessibilityInfo: Wheelchair division available. Accessible facilities at the start and finish.
events.parkingInfo: No event parking. Trains to Town Hall (start) and bus services back from Bondi. Road closures in effect from 06:00.
events.bagDropInfo: Bag buses transport gear from Hyde Park start to the Bondi Beach finish. Collected by bib number.
events.additionalNotes: Fancy dress strongly encouraged! Thousands of runners in costume make this the most colourful race in Australia. Dogs on leads welcome in the general wave.

events.title: Wodapalooza Australia
events.description: Premier CrossFit festival featuring competition across multiple divisions. RX, Scaled, and Masters categories.
events.fullDescription: Wodapalooza Australia is a multi-day CrossFit festival that brings together hundreds of athletes from across the country to compete, connect, and celebrate the sport. Inspired by the iconic Miami event, this Australian edition hosts multiple individual and team divisions with programming designed to challenge athletes at every level.
events.workoutDescription: Multi-day event with 4–5 programmed workouts per division. Programming is released progressively. Expect barbell cycling, gymnastics, cardio equipment, and functional fitness challenges. Teams complete all workouts together.
events.transferPolicy: Athlete substitutions in Teams permitted up to 21 days before the event. Division upgrades subject to availability.
events.refundPolicy: Refund less 20% admin fee up to 60 days before. No refund within 60 days. Credits available.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Competition entry, Wodapalooza event t-shirt, athlete wristband, judging, two-day access to the athlete floor.
events.optionalExtras: VIP hospitality passes ($120), merchandise bundles from $50, spectator day passes ($25/day).
events.groupDiscount: Affiliate boxes entering 5+ athletes across any division receive a 10% rebate. Contact affiliates@wodapalooza.com.au.
events.charityComponent: Wodapalooza Australia supports Movember. Athletes can fundraise through the event platform.
events.prizeStructure: Podium prizes and Wodapalooza trophies for top 3 in each division. RX Individual division offers prize money.
events.specialAwards: Affiliate of the Year, Spirit of the Sport, Judges' Choice
events.expoDetails: Full vendor expo running across both days. 50+ brands, supplement companies, apparel, and gym equipment. Free for spectators.
events.bibCollectionInfo: Athlete check-in: 15 May 2026 (Friday) 16:00–20:00 at the Showgrounds main entrance. Mandatory check-in required.
events.athleteBriefing: Division briefings held on Day 1 morning before competition begins. Schedule published 1 week prior.
events.accessibilityInfo: Melbourne Showgrounds is accessible. Contact organisers for specific accommodation requirements.
events.parkingInfo: Showgrounds parking available ($15/day). Flemington and Kensington stations nearby.
events.bagDropInfo: Athlete bag area on the competition floor. Access during your division's warm-up window only.
events.additionalNotes: Multi-day event — athletes must be available for all scheduled heats across both days. Schedule released 2 weeks prior.

events.title: Perth Running Festival
events.description: Western Australia's major running event. Marathon, half marathon, 12km and 4km distances along the Swan River.
events.fullDescription: The Perth Running Festival is WA's most celebrated running event, taking participants on a stunning journey along the Swan River foreshore. From the elite marathon to the family-friendly 4km, there is a distance for every runner. The flat, scenic course is perfect for first-timers and those chasing a personal best.
events.workoutDescription: Flat road race along the Swan River. Marathon and half marathon are out-and-back along the river foreshore. The 12km winds through South Perth and Kings Park. The 4km is an out-and-back suitable for all fitness levels.
events.transferPolicy: Distance transfers available for a $10 fee up to 30 days before the event.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferrals to 2027 available for $20 up to 30 days before the event.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race refreshments.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($30), official photos ($45).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 10+ save 10%. Contact info@perthrunningfestival.com.au.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies for top 3 male/female in all distances. Age group recognition across all events.
events.specialAwards: First Perth Resident, Oldest Finisher, Best Costume
events.bibCollectionInfo: Bib collection: 23 May 2026 at Perth Convention Exhibition Centre, 10:00–17:00. Race day collection from 05:15.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide available online from 17 May. No mandatory on-site briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Flat, accessible course. Wheelchair-friendly facilities throughout.
events.parkingInfo: Limited parking on Riverside Drive. Recommend train to Perth Station or East Perth Station.
events.bagDropInfo: Free bag drop near the start line. Collected at the finish.
events.additionalNotes: Stunning Swan River sunrise for early starters. Bring sunscreen — can get warm by mid-morning.

events.title: Athlete Games
events.description: Hybrid fitness competition testing strength, endurance, and skill. Individual and team divisions across multiple events.
events.fullDescription: The Athlete Games is South Australia's premier hybrid fitness competition. Athletes are tested across four signature events over one day — a test of strength, endurance, skill, and mental toughness. The event welcomes all fitness levels and celebrates the all-round athlete.
events.workoutDescription: 4 events across the day combining: a 3km obstacle run, a max barbell complex, a team relay, and a timed fitness circuit. Full movement standards released 3 weeks before the event. Scaled options available in the Individual Open division.
events.transferPolicy: Athlete name changes accepted up to 2 weeks before the event for $10.
events.refundPolicy: 50% refund up to 30 days before the event. No refunds after that. Credits available.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Competition entry, event t-shirt, post-event lunch, timing and judging.
events.optionalExtras: Spectator tickets ($20), event hoodie ($60).
events.groupDiscount: 5% off for groups of 4+. Contact info@theatletegames.com.au.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies and sponsor prizes for podium in each division.
events.specialAwards: Athlete of the Day, Best Team Name, Fan Favourite
events.bibCollectionInfo: Athlete check-in from 07:00 on race day at the main entrance. Bring photo ID.
events.athleteBriefing: Athlete briefing at 07:45. Attendance mandatory. Safety standards and event flow covered.
events.accessibilityInfo: Adelaide Showground has accessible facilities. Contact organiser in advance for specific needs.
events.parkingInfo: Free on-site parking at the Adelaide Showground.
events.bagDropInfo: Unsupervised athlete bag area inside the venue.
events.additionalNotes: ANZAC Day event — the atmosphere is always special. Bring warm layers for early morning.

events.title: Brisbane Marathon Festival
events.description: Run through Brisbane's river city. Flat, fast course perfect for PBs. Multiple distances available.
events.fullDescription: The Brisbane Marathon Festival offers a stunning flat, fast course along the Brisbane River and through the CBD parklands. Perfect conditions for personal bests, this event attracts both elite runners and casual participants. South Bank Parklands hosts the bustling start/finish festival area.
events.workoutDescription: Flat road race starting and finishing at South Bank Parklands. Marathon takes in both sides of the river via Story Bridge. Half marathon is a single loop through South Brisbane and the CBD. 5km is a flat river-front route.
events.transferPolicy: Distance transfers available up to 4 weeks before the event for $15.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferral to 2027 for $25 up to 6 weeks before the event.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race refreshments.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($28), race photos ($40).
events.groupDiscount: Running clubs of 15+ save 10%.
events.prizeStructure: Prize money for top 3 male/female marathon finishers. Trophies for age group winners.
events.specialAwards: Course Record Bonus, First Brisbane Resident, Oldest Marathon Finisher
events.bibCollectionInfo: South Bank Cultural Forecourt: 5–6 Jun, 10:00–17:00. Race day collection from 05:00.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide published online 2 weeks before the event.
events.accessibilityInfo: Flat, accessible course. Wheelchair competitors welcome.
events.parkingInfo: South Bank carparks open from 04:30. Public transport strongly recommended — Roma Street Station nearby.
events.bagDropInfo: Free gear check at South Bank. Buses return gear to the finish festival.
events.additionalNotes: June in Brisbane is perfect running weather — cool mornings, low humidity. Great conditions for fast times.

events.title: Fittest in Cape
events.description: CrossFit throwdown at CrossFit Cape. Beginner-friendly comp with Scaled, RX, and Masters divisions.
events.fullDescription: Fittest in Cape is a community-focused CrossFit throwdown perfect for first-time competitors and experienced athletes alike. Hosted at CrossFit Cape, this one-day event features three fun, challenging workouts with an emphasis on great sportsmanship and community spirit.
events.workoutDescription: 3 workouts throughout the day. Beginner-friendly movements with scaled options for all. Typical workouts include dumbbell cycling, box jumps, row, bike, and bodyweight gymnastics. No barbell snatching or muscle-ups in Scaled division.
events.transferPolicy: Name transfers accepted up to 1 week before the event at no charge.
events.refundPolicy: Full refund up to 30 days before the event. 50% refund within 30 days. No refunds within 2 weeks.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Competition entry, event t-shirt, lunch provided, judging.
events.optionalExtras: Extra guest lunch tickets ($15), wristbands for spectators (free).
events.prizeStructure: Trophies for top 3 in each division. Spot prizes and raffles throughout the day.
events.specialAwards: Best First-Timer, Crowd Favourite, Loudest Supporter
events.bibCollectionInfo: Check-in from 07:30 on race day at the CrossFit Cape front desk.
events.athleteBriefing: Athlete briefing at 07:45 — mandatory. Standards and heat draws confirmed.
events.accessibilityInfo: Ground-level venue. Please advise the organiser of any specific accessibility needs.
events.parkingInfo: Free on-site parking available.
events.bagDropInfo: Unattended bag area inside the gym.
events.additionalNotes: Great beginner competition. Coaches and judges are supportive and encouraging. Bring a friend!

events.title: HYROX Brisbane
events.description: HYROX comes to Brisbane. Experience the world series of fitness racing in Queensland's capital.
events.fullDescription: HYROX Brisbane brings the world's most exciting fitness race to Queensland for the first time. The Brisbane Entertainment Centre is transformed into a purpose-built race venue where thousands of athletes compete side by side through 8km of running and 8 functional workout stations.
events.workoutDescription: 8 functional workout stations each preceded by a 1km run: SkiErg (1000m), Sled Push (50m), Sled Pull (50m), Burpee Broad Jumps (80m), Rowing (1000m), Farmers Carry (200m), Sandbag Lunges (100m), Wall Balls (100 reps). Total: 8km running + stations.
events.transferPolicy: Category transfers permitted up to 14 days before race day. $15 admin fee.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferrals to the following year's event for $25 up to 30 days before.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race recovery bag, expo access.
events.optionalExtras: Official race kit ($65), race photos ($45), merchandise bundle ($89).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 10+ receive 10% off.
events.charityComponent: HYROX Brisbane supports the Children's Hospital Foundation. Fundraise through your race profile.
events.prizeStructure: Cash and trophies for top 3 in each division. Age group trophies for all category winners.
events.ceremonyLocation: Main Stage, Brisbane Entertainment Centre — on-site
events.specialAwards: Segment KOM/QOM, Community Spirit Award
events.expoDetails: Free expo open to all. Brand activations, nutrition stands, and community activities.
events.bibCollectionInfo: 8 May 2026 (Day Before) — BEC Foyer, 10:00–18:00. Race day pick-up from 05:30.
events.athleteBriefing: Online briefing available from 3 May at hyrox.com/brisbane-briefing. Relay team briefing on-site at 06:30.
events.accessibilityInfo: Brisbane Entertainment Centre is fully accessible.
events.parkingInfo: On-site parking at the BEC. $15 flat rate. Train: Boondall Station is a 10-minute walk.
events.bagDropInfo: Free bag drop from 06:00 at the event village.
events.additionalNotes: Queensland's first HYROX — expect a sellout. Book accommodation early.

events.title: Canberra Marathon
events.description: Run the nation's capital. Ultra, marathon, half marathon, and 10km through Canberra's parklands.
events.fullDescription: The Canberra Marathon is one of Australia's most beautiful running events, taking runners through the landscaped parklands, lake shores, and tree-lined avenues of the nation's capital. The crisp autumn air and flat course make it a favourite for personal bests. From the 10km to the 50km Ultra, there's a distance for every level.
events.workoutDescription: Flat road course through Canberra's parliamentary and lakeside precincts. Marathon and half marathon are out-and-back loops around Lake Burley Griffin. Ultra runs 2 marathon-length loops. Beautiful autumn foliage lines the course.
events.transferPolicy: Distance changes available up to 6 weeks before for $15.
events.refundPolicy: Deferrals to 2027 for $20 up to 30 days before the event. No refunds.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race morning tea.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($30), race photos ($40), pre-event pasta dinner ($35).
events.groupDiscount: Running clubs of 10+ save 10%.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies and prize vouchers for top 3 male/female in each distance. Age group trophies.
events.specialAwards: First Canberra Resident, Best Costume, Oldest Finisher
events.bibCollectionInfo: Bib pick-up: 11 Apr (Saturday) at Commonwealth Park, 09:00–16:00. Race day collection from 05:00.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide published at canberramarathon.com.au. No mandatory briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Flat, paved course. Wheelchair-accessible facilities throughout Commonwealth Park.
events.parkingInfo: Parking in Commonwealth Park and adjacent areas. Public transport — buses to the city and a short walk.
events.bagDropInfo: Free gear check at Commonwealth Park festival area.
events.additionalNotes: Autumn in Canberra is stunning — the foliage along the course is world-class. Bring layers for the early morning start.

events.title: Raw Challenge
events.description: Obstacle course race through natural terrain. 6km, 12km, and 24km courses with 20+ obstacles.
events.fullDescription: Raw Challenge is Australia's original mud and obstacle run, held at the spectacular Glenworth Valley in NSW. Competitors battle through creeks, mud pits, rope climbs, cargo nets, and bushland trails across three course distances. Whether you're tackling it solo or with a team of mates, Raw Challenge is equal parts brutal and brilliant.
events.workoutDescription: Outdoor obstacle course through natural bushland, creek crossings, and open paddocks. 20+ obstacles including: mud crawl, rope wall, tyre flips, suspended monkey bars, 4m cargo net, creek swims, and the infamous mud pit finish. All obstacles have bypass options.
events.transferPolicy: Distance changes and name transfers accepted up to 2 weeks before for $10.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds within 30 days. 50% refund up to 60 days before.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Race bib, timing chip, finisher medal, post-race hose-down station, complimentary sausage sizzle.
events.optionalExtras: Event t-shirt ($35), camping at Glenworth Valley ($30/night), professional photos ($40).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 5+ save 10%. Corporate team packages available.
events.charityComponent: Raw Challenge partners with Surf Life Saving Australia. Athletes can fundraise online.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies for top 3 male/female in each course distance. Team trophies for the fastest team.
events.specialAwards: Hardest Worker, Most Creative Costume, First-Timer Award
events.bibCollectionInfo: Check-in on race day from 07:00 at the event entrance. No pre-race collection needed.
events.athleteBriefing: Safety briefing at the start line before each wave. No prior attendance required.
events.accessibilityInfo: Course contains significant rough terrain. Not suitable for wheelchair users. Modified shorter course available — contact organisers.
events.parkingInfo: Free on-site parking at Glenworth Valley. Shuttle buses from Gosford Station available — book in advance.
events.bagDropInfo: Secure bag drop at the start/finish area. Store valuables in your car.
events.additionalNotes: Wear clothes you don't mind destroying. Bring a change of clothes and towel. Creek water temperature can be cold in June.

events.title: Hobart Running Festival
events.description: Tasmania's premier running event along the Derwent River. Marathon, half marathon, 10km and 5km.
events.fullDescription: The Hobart Running Festival is Tasmania's flagship running event, showcasing the natural beauty of the Derwent River waterfront. The flat, fast course winds through Hobart's historic waterfront district and along the river foreshore, making it one of Australia's most scenic running experiences. Perfect conditions for autumn PBs.
events.workoutDescription: Flat road race along the Derwent River foreshore. The marathon takes in both river banks via the Tasman Bridge. Half marathon is a waterfront loop past Salamanca Place. 5km is a flat out-and-back perfect for beginners.
events.transferPolicy: Distance changes available up to 4 weeks before for $10.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferrals to 2027 for $15 up to 30 days before.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race refreshments.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($25), race photos ($35).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 8+ save 10%.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies for top 3 in all distances. Age group recognition.
events.specialAwards: First Tasmanian, Best Dressed, Oldest Finisher
events.bibCollectionInfo: Salamanca Markets precinct: 30 May, 10:00–16:00. Race day collection from 05:30.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide published online. No mandatory briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Flat waterfront course. Accessible facilities available.
events.parkingInfo: Waterfront parking available but limited. Recommend taxi or rideshare.
events.bagDropInfo: Free bag check near Constitution Dock.
events.additionalNotes: Hobart in May is cold and crisp — ideal racing conditions. Bring warm gear for the post-race wait.

events.title: Chaos Games
events.description: Hybrid fitness competition with unknown workouts revealed on the day. Test your all-round fitness.
events.fullDescription: Chaos Games is Melbourne's most exciting hybrid fitness competition. The concept is simple — no programming preview. Workouts are revealed on the day and designed to test all aspects of physical fitness: strength, power, endurance, agility, and mental fortitude. If you think you're fit, come find out.
events.workoutDescription: 4 unknown workouts across the day. Previous years have included: max rep barbell complex, 2km assault bike, tyre flip relay, rope climbs and sprints, and a mystery endurance finale. Scaled options available in the Open division only. Full standards revealed on race morning.
events.transferPolicy: Name transfers accepted up to 1 week before for $10.
events.refundPolicy: 50% refund up to 30 days before. No refunds within 30 days.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Competition entry, event t-shirt, athlete lunch, timing.
events.optionalExtras: Event hoodie ($70), extra guest lunch tickets ($20).
events.prizeStructure: Trophies and sponsor prizes for top 3 in each division. Overall champion receives a $500 prize.
events.specialAwards: Most Fearless Athlete, Crowd Favourite, Best Newcomer
events.bibCollectionInfo: Check-in from 07:15 on race day.
events.athleteBriefing: Standards briefing at 07:45 after workout reveal. Mandatory attendance.
events.accessibilityInfo: Ground-level warehouse venue. Contact organiser for specific needs.
events.parkingInfo: Street parking on Westgate St and surrounding streets. Footscray Station nearby.
events.bagDropInfo: Athlete bag area inside the venue.
events.additionalNotes: Truly unknown programming — athletes cannot prepare specifically. Just bring your fitness.

events.title: Run Melbourne
events.description: Half marathon and 10km through Melbourne's streets. Fast, flat course ideal for beginners and PB chasers.
events.fullDescription: Run Melbourne is Victoria's mid-winter running festival, transforming the CBD streets into a race course for tens of thousands of runners. The iconic 21.1km half marathon takes in Melbourne's best landmarks — the MCG, AAMI Park, Albert Park Lake, and South Melbourne — before finishing in the heart of the CBD.
events.streetAddress: Start: Princes Bridge, Finish: MCG Forecourt
events.workoutDescription: Flat road race through Melbourne's inner suburbs. Half marathon takes in Albert Park and South Melbourne. 10km is a CBD city loop. 5km is a flat out-and-back from the MCG. All courses are certified and fast.
events.transferPolicy: Distance changes available up to 6 weeks before for $15.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferrals for $20 up to 4 weeks before the event.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race refreshments.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($30), official photos ($45), guaranteed start wave ($20).
events.groupDiscount: Running clubs of 15+ save 10%.
events.charityComponent: The Age Run Melbourne charity program supports 30+ charities. Charity bibs available with $500 fundraising minimum.
events.prizeStructure: Prize money for top 5 male/female half marathon. Age group trophies for all distances.
events.specialAwards: First Time Half Marathon Finisher of the Year, Fastest Corporate Team
events.expoDetails: Run Melbourne Expo at the MCG, 24–25 Jul. Bib pick-up, sponsor stands, running product launches.
events.bibCollectionInfo: MCG Gate 3: 24 Jul (10:00–18:00), 25 Jul (09:00–17:00). Race day bib collection not available.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide published at runmelbourne.com.au. No mandatory briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Flat, accessible course. Wheelchair division available for the half marathon.
events.parkingInfo: MCG carpark available from 05:30. Flinders Street and Richmond stations nearby.
events.bagDropInfo: Free gear check at the MCG. Drop-off near start, collected at the finish.
events.additionalNotes: Melbourne winter racing weather is ideal — cool and consistent. Pacer groups for 1:45, 2:00, 2:15, and 2:30 half marathon targets.

events.title: HYROX Perth
events.description: Western Australia's HYROX debut. The global fitness race comes to Perth for the first time.
events.fullDescription: HYROX Perth marks Western Australia's entry into the world's most popular fitness racing series. The Perth Convention Centre provides an exceptional race venue where athletes from across WA and interstate will compete through 8km of running and 8 signature HYROX workout stations.
events.workoutDescription: 8 functional workout stations each preceded by a 1km run: SkiErg (1000m), Sled Push (50m), Sled Pull (50m), Burpee Broad Jumps (80m), Rowing (1000m), Farmers Carry (200m), Sandbag Lunges (100m), Wall Balls (100 reps). Total: 8km running + stations.
events.transferPolicy: Category transfers permitted up to 14 days before race day. $15 admin fee.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferrals available for $25 up to 30 days before.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race recovery bag, expo access.
events.optionalExtras: Official race kit ($65), race photos ($45), merchandise bundle ($89).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 10+ receive 10% off.
events.charityComponent: HYROX Perth supports the Telethon Kids Institute. Fundraise through your race profile.
events.prizeStructure: Cash and trophies for top 3 in each division.
events.specialAwards: Segment KOM/QOM, First WA Finisher, Community Spirit Award
events.expoDetails: Free expo open to all. WA debut brings extra special community programming and sponsor activations.
events.bibCollectionInfo: 21 Aug 2026 (Day Before) — Convention Centre foyer, 10:00–18:00. Race day pick-up from 05:30.
events.athleteBriefing: Online briefing from 16 Aug at hyrox.com/perth-briefing. Relay briefing on-site at 06:30.
events.accessibilityInfo: Perth Convention Centre is fully accessible.
events.parkingInfo: Convention Centre car park open from 05:30. Train: Perth Station is a 10-minute walk.
events.bagDropInfo: Free bag drop from 06:00.
events.additionalNotes: WA's first HYROX event — expected to sell out fast. Register early to avoid disappointment.

events.title: Cairns Marathon
events.description: Run through tropical North Queensland. Marathon, half marathon and 10km along the Esplanade.
events.fullDescription: The Cairns Marathon is a bucket-list running event set in tropical North Queensland. The flat, fast course along the world-famous Cairns Esplanade and through the city's lush green foreshore is a stunning setting for racing. Cool sea breezes, spectacular views of the Coral Sea, and an incredible post-race atmosphere make this one of Australia's most loved marathons.
events.workoutDescription: Flat road race along the Cairns Esplanade and foreshore. Marathon takes in a loop of the northern beaches before returning to the Esplanade finish. Half marathon and 10km are point-to-point along the waterfront with views of the Coral Sea.
events.transferPolicy: Distance transfers for $10 up to 4 weeks before the event.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferrals to 2027 for $20 up to 30 days before.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race tropical refreshments.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($30), official race photos ($45).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 8+ save 10%.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies and prize vouchers for top 3 male/female. Age group awards.
events.specialAwards: First Cairns Local, Best International Participant, Oldest Finisher
events.bibCollectionInfo: Esplanade Lagoon area: 13 Jun, 10:00–17:00. Race day from 04:30.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide published online. No mandatory briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Flat esplanade course. Accessible facilities throughout.
events.parkingInfo: Esplanade car parks open from 04:00. Cairns CBD is walkable from most accommodation.
events.bagDropInfo: Free bag check at the event finish area.
events.additionalNotes: Early June start time (05:30) keeps temperatures manageable. Still warm — expect humidity. Hydrate well the day before.

events.title: The Open Throwdown
events.description: CrossFit Open-style competition. Complete the Open workouts in a live competition atmosphere.
events.fullDescription: The Open Throwdown at CrossFit 3018 brings the energy of the CrossFit Open to life in a live competition format. Compete alongside friends and rivals on the Open workouts in real time, with a crowd, judges, and a festival atmosphere. Perfect for athletes who want the competition experience without a full sanctioned comp.
events.workoutDescription: Complete the current CrossFit Open week's workout in a live competition environment. Each athlete completes the workout once with a dedicated judge. Scores submitted to the official CrossFit Open leaderboard. Same standards as the global Open.
events.transferPolicy: Full name transfers accepted up to 1 week before at no charge.
events.refundPolicy: Full refund up to 14 days before. No refunds within 14 days.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Event entry, dedicated judge, t-shirt, post-event food and drinks.
events.optionalExtras: Spectator tickets (free — all welcome).
events.prizeStructure: Spot prizes and raffle for all participants. Top 3 in each division receive trophies.
events.specialAwards: Best Effort, Loudest Crowd, CrossFit Open Spirit Award
events.bibCollectionInfo: Check-in from 08:30 at CrossFit 3018 reception.
events.athleteBriefing: Briefing at 08:50. Movement standards reviewed before heats begin.
events.accessibilityInfo: Ground floor venue. Contact the gym for specific accessibility needs.
events.parkingInfo: Street parking on Lorimer St. Docklands tram stops nearby.
events.bagDropInfo: Unsupervised bag area in the gym.
events.additionalNotes: CrossFit Open registration required before competing ($20 via CrossFit.com). Bring your CrossFit ID for score submission.

events.title: Spartan Race Sydney
events.description: Obstacle course racing with Sprint (5km), Super (10km), and Beast (21km) distances available.
events.fullDescription: Spartan Race is the world's number one obstacle course racing brand, and the Sydney edition at the International Regatta Centre delivers some of the most challenging and rewarding race experiences in Australia. Choose your distance — Sprint, Super, or Beast — and take on 20–30 brutal obstacles designed to test every facet of your fitness.
events.workoutDescription: Signature Spartan obstacles including: barbed wire crawl, 8-foot wall, atlas stone carry, rope climb, spear throw, Hercules hoist, bucket brigade, Z-wall, and the finish line fire jump. 30 burpee penalty for failed obstacles. Obstacles vary by distance.
events.transferPolicy: Race transfers between distances available online up to 7 days before for $15. Wave transfers for $5.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Race insurance available at checkout. Credit to a future event available for $25 up to 30 days before.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Race entry, Spartan finisher medal, banana and water at finish. Elite heat athletes receive trifecta medal piece.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($35), official race photos ($55), gear check ($10), Spartan Kids Race entry ($30).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 5+ save 10% with code TEAM10. Corporate challenges available.
events.charityComponent: Spartan Australia partners with Beyond Blue. Spartan for a Cause bibs available — no fundraising minimum.
events.prizeStructure: Prize money and Spartan trophies for top 3 elite male/female in each distance.
events.specialAwards: Trifecta Tribe recognition, Fastest Local Athlete, Spirit of the Spartan
events.expoDetails: Spartan Festival Village on-site with gear, supplements, food vendors, and kids activities. Free entry for spectators.
events.bibCollectionInfo: Race day check-in only — from 06:00 at the event entrance. Arrive early as queues build quickly.
events.athleteBriefing: Pre-race wave briefings held at the start line 10 minutes before each wave. Obstacles and safety rules covered.
events.minAge: 14+ for all distances (under 18 with parental consent). Under 14 can race the Spartan Kids course.
events.accessibilityInfo: Obstacle course terrain is inherently uneven. Some obstacles can be bypassed. Contact accessibility@spartan.com for specific needs.
events.parkingInfo: Free on-site parking at the Regatta Centre. Penrith Station is a 20-minute taxi ride.
events.bagDropInfo: Gear check available for $10 near the start/finish. All valuables at your own risk.
events.additionalNotes: Wear clothes and shoes you don't mind getting destroyed. Gloves recommended for rope climbs. Bring a change of clothes and a towel.

events.title: Adelaide Marathon
events.description: South Australia's major marathon event. Run through Adelaide's parklands and city streets.
events.fullDescription: The Adelaide Marathon is South Australia's showcase running event, taking participants through the elegant parklands, riverside trails, and city streets that make Adelaide one of Australia's most liveable cities. Starting and finishing at the iconic Adelaide Oval, this event combines world-class facilities with a beautiful scenic course.
events.workoutDescription: Flat to undulating road race starting and finishing at Adelaide Oval. Marathon takes in the River Torrens Linear Trail, Velopark, and the eastern parklands. Half marathon is a riverfront loop. 5km is an entry-level riverside route suitable for all fitness levels.
events.transferPolicy: Distance changes available for $10 up to 4 weeks before the event.
events.refundPolicy: No refunds. Deferrals to 2027 for $15 up to 30 days before.
events.entryFeeInclusions: Finisher medal, timing chip, race bib, post-race refreshments.
events.optionalExtras: Finisher t-shirt ($28), race photos ($40), pre-race pasta dinner ($30).
events.groupDiscount: Groups of 10+ save 10%. Contact groups@adelaidemarathon.com.au.
events.charityComponent: Official charity partner: Hutt St Centre. Athletes can set up a fundraising page through the event website.
events.prizeStructure: Trophies and prize vouchers for top 3 male/female in all distances. Age group recognition.
events.specialAwards: First SA Resident, Fastest Club Athlete, Best Fundraiser
events.bibCollectionInfo: Adelaide Oval Gate 5: 16 May (10:00–17:00). Race day collection from 05:30.
events.athleteBriefing: Race guide published at adelaidemarathon.com.au. No mandatory briefing.
events.accessibilityInfo: Mostly flat, accessible course. Wheelchair competitors welcome in the marathon.
events.parkingInfo: Adelaide Oval carparks open from 05:00. Adelaide Train Station is a 10-minute walk.
events.bagDropInfo: Free gear check at Adelaide Oval. Identified by bib number.
events.additionalNotes: May in Adelaide is ideal running weather — cool and dry. One of Australia's friendliest running communities.
